claim: Rhythmic expression of Cry1 translates directly into a circadian regulation of cAMP signaling in gluconeogenesis.
evidence_sentences: Here we show that Creb activity during fasting is modulated by Cry1 and Cry2, which are rhythmically expressed in the liver.

Cry1 expression was elevated during the night-day transition, when it reduced fasting gluconeogenic gene expression by blocking glucagon-mediated increases in intracellular cAMP concentrations and in the protein kinase A-mediated phosphorylation of Creb.
